main_turf.bias_notes: Clockwise. A notoriously tight provincial track on the Central Coast. Its defining feature is a very short 250m home straight. Because the final sprint is so brief and the turns are tight, the track naturally creates a massive bias toward front-runners ('E' types) and horses drawn inside. Sweeping closers frequently run out of time and ground.
main_turf.post_bias: Inside barriers (1-4) are highly advantageous. Horses drawn wide face a severe statistical penalty as they are often forced to work hard early to cross over, or risk being caught wide on the continuous tight turns.

handicapping_angles.the_bullring_trap: Aggressively downgrade deep closers ('Swoopers'). With only 250 metres of straight runway, horses settling in the back half of the field will almost always run out of ground. Heavily upgrade pure early speed and rail-skimming tactical stalkers.
handicapping_angles.provincial_metropolitan_bridge: Gosford often hosts high-quality provincial fields and off-season Metropolitan runners. Form from tighter Sydney tracks like Canterbury often translates perfectly to Gosford's tight geometry.
handicapping_angles.wide_barrier_penalty: Apply a strict downgrade to horses drawn wide (barrier 9+). Due to the tight circumference (1,710m), ground loss on the turns is highly detrimental and very difficult to recover from.
